title: Kunafa with cheese
subtitle: Levantine shredded pastry with warm cheese and syrup
excerpt: Kunafa (knafeh) is a Levantine baked pastry made from thin shredded dough layered over a mild white cheese, baked until the pastry is crisp and the cheese melts, then drenched in orange blossom syrup. It is served immediately, hot, so the cheese pulls in long strings when cut. The shredded dough is sold ready-made as kadayif.
sourceNotes: Kunafa is one of the most widely consumed pastries in the Levant and Egypt, with documented history in Arab and Ottoman culinary texts from the medieval period. The Nablus variety, made with a salty, stretchy cheese called Nabulsi and coloured orange with food colouring or annatto, is the most famous regional version. Abroad, unsalted mozzarella or a mixture of ricotta and mozzarella is the standard substitute for Nabulsi cheese. The orange colour is optional; the cheese and syrup combination is the defining characteristic.

recipe.makeAheadNotes: The syrup can be made up to 3 days ahead. Assemble and bake just before serving: kunafa does not hold well once made.

baking.bakeTemperatureNote: fan oven; 30 to 38 minutes until the pastry base is crisp and deep gold; the cheese will be melted and bubbling at the edges

content.text: Kadayif (also sold as kataifi) is available from Middle Eastern and Turkish grocers and many larger supermarkets. It looks like very fine, dry noodles and is sold in a block. Before using, pull it apart into loose strands and rub generously with melted butter so every strand is coated. The kunafa is assembled upside-down: the pastry goes in first, then the cheese, then it is inverted onto a plate after baking so the crisp pastry is on top.

Make the syrup first: bring 250 g caster sugar, 150 ml water, and the juice of half a lemon to a boil and simmer for 5 minutes. Stir in 1 tablespoon of orange blossom water and leave to cool. Heat the oven to 190 degrees C fan. Butter a 28 cm round tin or a 30 x 20 cm rectangular tin well. Pull apart 400 g kadayif pastry into loose strands in a large bowl and pour over 150 g melted butter. Rub and toss until every strand is coated. Press two-thirds of the pastry into the base of the tin in an even layer, compressing it firmly with your palms. Spread the grated mozzarella (mixed with ricotta if using) evenly over the pastry. Top with the remaining pastry and press down again firmly.

Bake for 30 to 38 minutes until the top is a deep, even gold and the cheese is visibly melted and bubbling at the edges. Remove from the oven and immediately invert onto a large serving plate or board: hold the plate firmly against the tin and flip in one confident movement. Pour half the cold syrup evenly over the top. Scatter with chopped pistachios. Serve immediately, cut into portions, with the remaining syrup on the side.

items.problem: The pastry did not crisp and is soft
items.solution: The butter coating was uneven, or the pastry layer was too thick and not compressed firmly enough. Every strand must be buttered, and the base layer needs to be pressed down so it makes good contact with the tin.

items.problem: The cheese has leaked out and burnt on the tin
items.solution: The cheese layer was too thick or too wet. Mozzarella varies in moisture content: drain it on kitchen paper for 10 minutes before using, and keep the layer no thicker than 1.5 cm.
